name: Executive Resource ODE with Dual-Value Arbitration
description: Continuous-time ODE governing depletable executive (dlPFC) resource dynamics, combined with a dual-system architecture where an impulsive controller (taste-driven, Pavlovian/habitual) competes with a goal-directed controller (health-integrated). An arbitration weight derived from executive capacity determines the mixture proportion. When executive resources are critically low, the agent suffers complete self-control failure and is forced to follow the impulsive system. Habit values are learned via model-free temporal difference updating. Based on Rangel et al. (2008) three-controller architecture and Rangel (2013) postulates P2 and P4. Since the environment only provides 'palatability' on food resources, health is derived as (1 - palatability), creating the taste-health conflict that drives dual-system arbitration.

variables.name: executive_capacity
variables.description: Depletable dlPFC resource level. 1 = full capacity, 0 = exhausted. Governs the agent's ability to engage goal-directed control over impulsive responses.

variables.name: impulsive_value
variables.description: Value of action a in state s from the Pavlovian/habitual controller. Driven purely by taste (palatability), distance-discounted. Updated via model-free TD learning.

variables.name: goal_directed_value
variables.description: Value of action a in state s from the model-based goal-directed controller. Integrates both taste and health attributes with respective weights.

variables.name: integrated_value
variables.description: Arbitrated mixture of impulsive and goal-directed action values, weighted by omega.

variables.name: arbitration_weight
variables.description: Proportion of goal-directed influence in the integrated value. Derived as E^2 to capture nonlinear collapse of self-control under severe depletion.

variables.name: control_exertion_flag
variables.description: Binary indicator of whether dlPFC is actively overriding the impulsive system this step. Set to 1 when the goal-directed and impulsive systems disagree on the best action.

parameters.name: depletion_rate
parameters.source: Knowledge backbone; cognitive depletion assumption from Rangel (2013)

parameters.name: recovery_rate
parameters.source: Knowledge backbone: recovery approximately half depletion rate

parameters.name: override_threshold
parameters.source: Derived from Rangel (2013) P4: minimum executive capacity needed for conflict override

parameters.name: stochastic_lapse_rate
parameters.source: Standard exploration/noise parameter in RL; accounts for motor execution noise

parameters.name: distance_discount_factor
parameters.source: Standard temporal/spatial discounting for approach value in grid environments

parameters.name: health_weight_goal_directed
parameters.source: Rangel (2013): goal-directed system weights health when engaged

rules.description: Executive capacity dynamics (Euler-discretized ODE). Recovery drives E toward 1 with rate beta_E; depletion drains E at rate alpha_E whenever ctrl=1 (dlPFC actively overriding impulse). Clamped to [0, 1].
rules.pseudocode:
# Continuous form: dE/dt = beta_E * (1 - E) - alpha_E * ctrl
# Discrete Euler step:
E_new = E + beta_E * (1.0 - E) - alpha_E * ctrl
E = clamp(E_new, 0.0, 1.0)

rules.description: Impulsive action value: Pavlovian/habitual value based purely on taste (palatability) of the nearest food resource accessible via action a, discounted by Manhattan distance. For eat at current position, distance = 0.
Goal-directed action value: integrates both taste and health attributes with explicit weights, discounted by distance. Health derived as (1 - palatability).
Arbitration weight: nonlinear mapping from executive capacity to goal-directed influence. Squaring ensures moderate depletion has small effects but severe depletion rapidly collapses self-control.
Integrated value: weighted mixture of goal-directed and impulsive action values.
rules.pseudocode:
for each action a:
    V[a] = omega * V_G[a] + (1.0 - omega) * V_I[a]

rules.description: Control exertion detection: conflict flag is 1 when the impulsive-best and goal-directed-best actions disagree.
rules.pseudocode:
best_impulsive = argmax(V_I)
best_goal_directed = argmax(V_G)
ctrl = 1 if best_impulsive != best_goal_directed else 0

rules.description: Habit value update via model-free TD: after eating, update cached Q-value for the (state, action) pair toward the received taste reward.
rules.pseudocode:
if last_action == 'eat' and successfully_ate:
    taste_reward = eaten_food_palatability
    state_key = discretize_state(last_position, nearby_food_config)
    Q_habit[(state_key, 'eat')] += alpha_Q * (taste_reward - Q_habit[(state_key, 'eat')])

decision_logic.description: Agent perceives grid position and visible food resources with palatability. Health is derived as (1 - palatability). Two parallel value systems compute action values: impulsive (taste-only) and goal-directed (taste+health weighted). Conflict between these triggers executive resource depletion. Arbitration weight from executive capacity determines the integrated value. Epsilon-greedy selection over integrated values, with forced impulsive fallback when executive capacity drops below threshold during conflict.
pseudocode: food_at_pos = [f for f in visible_foods if f.x == position[0] and f.y == position[1]]
# 2. If we ate last step, update habit values via R7
# 4. Compute impulsive values V_I for all actions via R2
# 5. Compute goal-directed values V_G for all actions via R3
ctrl = 1 if argmax(V_I) != argmax(V_G) else 0  # R6
E = clamp(E + beta_E * (1.0 - E) - alpha_E * ctrl, 0.0, 1.0)  # R1
V = {a: omega * V_G[a] + (1.0 - omega) * V_I[a] for a in candidate_actions}  # R5
# 10. Action selection: check for forced impulsive failure first
# Complete self-control failure per P3: forced impulsive action
# Normal greedy selection over integrated values

perception_to_variables.visible_foods: perception.resources.food  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_at_position: [f for f in perception.resources.food if f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y]
perception_to_variables.taste_of_food: food.palatability  # direct mapping from env property
perception_to_variables.ate_successfully: perception.last_action_result  # check consumed flag after eat action

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect reward (1.0 from env); agent internally distinguishes taste reward (palatability) from health value (1-palatability) for dual-system valuation

expected_behaviors.description: Executive capacity depletes during sustained conflict and recovers during non-conflict periods
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: place high-palatability and low-palatability foods creating conflict → run 40 steps → assert E < 0.6; then remove conflict foods → run 20 steps → assert E > previous E

expected_behaviors.description: When E is high, agent integrates health into decisions (prefers balanced or healthy food); when E is low, agent acts impulsively (prefers highest palatability)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set E=1.0 → assert agent sometimes chooses lower palatability food; set E=0.1 → assert agent almost always chooses highest palatability

expected_behaviors.description: Hard self-control failure: when E drops below theta during conflict, agent is forced to take impulsive action
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: deplete E to 0.2 (below theta=0.3) with conflict present → assert selected action == argmax(V_I) for 10 consecutive trials

expected_behaviors.description: Habit values update via TD learning: repeatedly eating high-palatability food strengthens the habit Q-value for that state-action pair
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: agent eats food with palatability 0.9 ten times at same location → assert Q_habit for that (state, eat) > 0.5

expected_behaviors.description: Arbitration weight omega exhibits nonlinear collapse: E=0.7 → omega=0.49 (moderate), E=0.3 → omega=0.09 (near-impulsive)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set E=0.7 → assert abs(omega - 0.49) < 0.01; set E=0.3 → assert abs(omega - 0.09) < 0.01

references: Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357
Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561
